name: New York Strip Steak with Sauteed Spinach Recipe
brief_description: Classic New York strip steaks seasoned simply with salt and pepper, grilled to perfection, and served over sautéed garlic spinach. A restaurant-quality steakhouse dinner that's naturally gluten-free and ready in under 30 minutes.

instructions.text: Rub salt and ground black pepper on all sides of the steak. Let stand for at least 10 minutes.
Grill the steak in medium heat for about 6 to 8 minutes per side.
Note: if you are using charcoal grill, try flipping the steak once in a while to prevent it from burning.
Remove the steak from the grill and let it rest for 15 minutes.
Meanwhile, heat a pan and pour-in the extra virgin olive oil.
Add the garlic and cook until the color turns light brown,
Put-in the spinach and sauté for 1 minute.
Turn off the heat and arrange the spinach in a large serving plate.
Put the grilled steak on top of the spinach.
Serve with mashed potato on the side along with your favorite steak sauce.

recipe_notes: For best results, bring steaks to room temperature 30 minutes before grilling.
Letting the steak rest for 15 minutes after grilling ensures the juices redistribute for a more tender result.
If using a charcoal grill, create a two-zone fire to move steaks away from flare-ups.
storage_notes: Store leftover steak and spinach separately in airtight containers in the refrigerator for up to 3 days.
Reheat steak gently in a low oven (275°F) to avoid overcooking.
